  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",

    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Make this stronger,” which primary focus should we take: (A) rhetorical polish and concision without changing structure or claims (R1.6), (B) sharpen the contribution and novelty positioning versus GraphFlow‑v1 and related approaches, possibly with light reordering (R1.1, R1.4, R1.5), or (C) tighten claims to evidence with calibrated language and uncertainty/significance cues (R1.3, RX.1–RX.2)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A stronger piece often requires re‑articulating the core thesis and gap to foreground the practical and conceptual contribution.",
    "R1.3": "To claim parameter efficiency and maintained accuracy convincingly, the text may need tighter linkage to the reported numbers and ablation.",
    "R1.4": "Perceived strength depends on clearly distinguishing GraphFlow from GraphFlow‑v1 and nearby approaches in terms of delta.",
    "R1.6": "More vigorous, concise phrasing and improved flow can materially enhance the perceived strength of the excerpt.",
    "RX.1": "Any strengthening should still disclose uncertainty (e.g., variance or significance) to avoid false confidence.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid overstating small improvements or generalizing beyond the evidence.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the effort within the bounds of strengthening this excerpt rather than adding new experiments or claims.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid language that could encourage risky or misleading deployment without adequate validation.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain explicit pointers to sections, datasets, and baselines so claims remain traceable after edits."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Make this stronger,” which primary focus should we take: (A) rhetorical polish and concision without changing structure or claims (R1.6), (B) sharpen the contribution and novelty positioning versus GraphFlow‑v1 and related approaches, possibly with light reordering (R1.1, R1.4, R1.5), or (C) tighten claims to evidence with calibrated language and uncertainty/significance cues (R1.3, RX.1–RX.2)?

rationale.R1.1: A stronger piece often requires re‑articulating the core thesis and gap to foreground the practical and conceptual contribution.
rationale.R1.3: To claim parameter efficiency and maintained accuracy convincingly, the text may need tighter linkage to the reported numbers and ablation.
rationale.R1.4: Perceived strength depends on clearly distinguishing GraphFlow from GraphFlow‑v1 and nearby approaches in terms of delta.
rationale.R1.6: More vigorous, concise phrasing and improved flow can materially enhance the perceived strength of the excerpt.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthening should still disclose uncertainty (e.g., variance or significance) to avoid false confidence.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid overstating small improvements or generalizing beyond the evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the effort within the bounds of strengthening this excerpt rather than adding new experiments or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid language that could encourage risky or misleading deployment without adequate validation.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain explicit pointers to sections, datasets, and baselines so claims remain traceable after edits.
